name: level-min-at-border
description: level_min stores a level number; level_raw 100 is exactly level 3's lower border, so level_min 3 passes and level_min 4 fails on both engines (spec §4).
subject: subject

name: level-max-exact-border-agree
description: At the exact lower border of the level (level_raw 100 = border of level 3) both level_max encodings agree (spec §4).
subject: subject

name: level-max-inside-level-divergence
description: level_raw 150 is level 3 (band [100, 250)) but above level 3's border: spec (level 3 <= level_max 3) says eligible; SQL (150 > border 100) says not a member.
subject: subject

sql.note: SQL compares level_raw 150 to border(level_max 3)=100; spec compares level numbers 3 <= 3 (eligibility-spec.md §4, §12 D-1)

name: level-max-zero-divergence
description: level_max 0 is a real bound per spec (only level-0 profiles pass) but the SQL builder drops zero bounds via truthiness, admitting everyone.
subject: subject

sql.note: SQL builder drops the falsy level_max 0 bound entirely; spec enforces level 1 > 0 (eligibility-spec.md §4, §12 D-3)

name: level-min-zero-noop
description: level_min 0 is vacuous on both engines (every profile is at least level 0; SQL also drops the falsy bound) - engines agree.
subject: subject
